system_context_diagram.notes: C4 Level 1 context diagram showing CLI, Orchestrator, Phase 01/03 agents, state.json

container_diagram.notes: Component interaction diagram showing CLI, Orchestrator, routing table, Phase 01/03 agents, artifact store

architecture_pattern_documented.notes: Prompt-driven agent architecture with generalized debate engine using routing table

major_components_identified.notes: 6 components: Generalized Debate Engine, Architecture Critic, Architecture Refiner, Creator Enhancement, Debate Artifacts, Edge Case Handling

component_responsibilities_defined.notes: Each component has defined inputs, outputs, and behavioral rules

backend_technology.notes: Markdown + YAML frontmatter for agent definitions; node:test CJS for tests. No new runtime code.

database_technology.notes: Prompt engineering feature -- no database (state.json is existing)

evaluation_criteria_documented.notes: Each technology choice has rationale and alternatives considered

database_design.notes: No database. State management uses existing state.json with additive debate_state field.

authorization_model.notes: Agent invocation authorization via advisory prompt-level controls

fail_safe_defaults.notes: 4 explicit fail-safe behaviors documented (Article X compliance)

threat_model.notes: 3 threat scenarios analyzed: prompt injection, unauthorized invocation, state corruption

architecture_pattern_adr.notes: Routing table vs per-phase sections vs sub-orchestrator

agent_routing_adr.notes: Phase-keyed lookup table vs config file vs convention-based

convergence_strategy_adr.notes: Reuse Phase 01 convergence (zero BLOCKING, max 3 rounds)

performance.notes: NFR-001: Sequential delegation with no parallel overhead; each agent runs within 5-minute budget

scalability.notes: Extensibility strategy: adding future phases requires one routing table entry

availability.notes: Edge case handling ensures debate never blocks workflow; fail-safe defaults

security.notes: 3 threat scenarios, 4 fail-safe defaults, STRIDE check built into Critic

backward_compatibility.notes: NFR-003: routing table produces identical Phase 01 agents; debate_mode=false = unchanged behavior

pattern_consistency.notes: NFR-002: Critic/Refiner follow identical structure to Phase 01 analogs

debate_loop_flow.notes: Mermaid flowchart showing generalized debate loop with routing table

sequence_diagram.notes: Mermaid sequence showing Phase 01 and Phase 03 routing side by side

component_diagram.notes: Mermaid graph showing all components and their interactions

critic_check_categories.notes: Mermaid graph showing 8 check categories and their artifact inputs

state_transitions.notes: Mermaid state diagram showing debate state machine

article_iii_security_by_design.notes: Security architecture defined. Critic AC-02 checks STRIDE completeness. 3 threats analyzed.

article_iv_explicit_over_implicit.notes: All assumptions documented. No NEEDS CLARIFICATION markers remain. All decisions have ADRs.

article_v_simplicity_first.notes: Single routing table, no sub-orchestrator, no new hooks, no new dependencies. Simplest approach that meets requirements.

article_vii_artifact_traceability.notes: Requirement traceability table in architecture-overview.md Section 9 maps all 7 FRs and 4 NFRs to architectural components.

article_ix_quality_gate_integrity.notes: All required artifacts exist. Gate validation report produced.

article_x_fail_safe_defaults.notes: 4 fail-safe defaults documented in security-architecture.md and architecture-overview.md Section 4.6.

summary: Phase 03 architecture design complete. 4 ADRs, 5 diagrams, security architecture with 3 threat analyses. Generalized debate engine with routing table approach (ADR-0001). All 7 FRs and 4 NFRs traced to architectural components. Zero constitutional violations.
